programme.title: Live: CBC Toronto News
programme.desc: Dwight Drummond and team take a comprehensive look at the top stories, with breaking news, reports and updates relevant to the city of Toronto.

programme.title: Live: NHL Hockey
programme.desc: The Canadiens face the Lightning in Game 2 of the East first round. Montreal leads 1-0 after a 4-3 win April 19. Juraj Slafkovsky had a hat trick, including the overtime winner; Brandon Hagel led Tampa Bay with two goals to force the extra period.

programme.title: Live: NHL Hockey
programme.desc: The Mammoth face the Golden Knights at T-Mobile Arena in Game 2 of the West first round. Vegas leads 1-0 after a 4-2 win. Logan Cooley scored the first playoff goal in Utah history while Mark Stone tied it and the Knights pulled the victory away.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: Live: CBC Toronto News at 11
programme.desc: The host presents information about the social, economic and political issues of the day and provides updates on current events in Toronto.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd's friends discuss their evidence against Theo, and Mal delivers a bombshell to Dev.

programme.title: Sort Of
programme.desc: Imran makes a surprise drop-in on Sabi; Bessy and Paul discover Henry is skipping school; 7ven and Wolf try to make a deal with Gaia to invest in the new club-gallery.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: One More Time
programme.desc: When "One More Time" introduces a new AI-powered skate sharpener, DJ struggles to convince the team and customers that the future isn't always best.

programme.title: Halifax Comedy Festival
programme.desc: Host Mark Critch welcomes stand-up comics Debra DiGiovanni, Mayce Galoni, Angelo Tsarouchas and Ted Morris.

programme.title: This Hour Has 22 Minutes
programme.desc: A look at the new year starring Aba Amuquandoh, Stacey McGunnigle, Trent McClellan, Chris Wilson and Mark Critch.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah tours the East Midlands in search of a property to suit today's buyers who, for the last 23 years, have been living in the USA.

programme.desc: What's going on in Canada and the world; a ten-minute dose of big news stories.

programme.title: Live: NHL Hockey
programme.desc: The Ducks face the Oilers in Game 2 of the West first round at Rogers Place. Edmonton won the opener 7-4 on Jan. 26, fueled by its power play. Connor McDavid leads the Oilers while Cutter Gauthier leads the Ducks. Anaheim looks to even the series.

programme.title: Live: CBC Toronto News
programme.desc: Dwight Drummond and team take a comprehensive look at the top stories, with breaking news, reports and updates relevant to the city of Toronto.

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: Based on an award-winning memoir from Mark Critch, "Son of a Critch" is the hilarious and very real story of a young Mark coming of age in the 1980s. Developed from Critch's 2018 memoir, which was in turn based on his real life experiences growing up in Newfoundland, Canada, the series is a heartfelt window in the life of a child who is much older inside than his 11 years. He uses comedy and self-deprecation to win friends and connect with the small collection of people in his limited junior high world.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Live: NHL Hockey
programme.desc: Eastern Conference First Round, Game 3.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.
